hints.vallis: 'vallis' is a noun meaning 'valley' — the subject
hints.impleo: 'impleo' is the 1st person singular present — 'implebitur' is the form used here

hints.to implore: 'to implore' is 'implorare' — 'implebitur' comes from 'implere' (to fill up)
hints.to employ: 'to employ' is a modern derivation — Latin 'implere' means 'to fill'

hints.Present passive: Present passive would be 'impletur' — the -bi- infix marks future
hints.Perfect passive: Perfect passive would be 'impletus est' — 'implebitur' is future passive
hints.Imperfect passive: Imperfect passive would be 'implebatur' — 'implebitur' has -bi- (future), not -ba- (imperfect)

hints.placo, placare, placavi, placatum: These are the parts of 'placare' (to appease)
hints.imploro, implorare, imploravi, imploratu: These are the parts of 'implorare' (to implore)
hints.compleo, complere, complevi, completum: These are the parts of 'complere' (to fill up) — similar but different prefix

hints.Genitive: Genitive singular is also 'vallis' (same form), but here it is the subject — nominative
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'vallem' — 'vallis' is nominative, subject of 'implebitur'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'vallis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'vallis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'vallis' ends in -is
